prerequisites: A running Airbyte instance (Open Source or Cloud) in which to configure the source.
No external account, API key, or credential is required. The E2E Testing source generates synthetic mock data and does not connect to any external system.
An understanding of the test data you want to produce, since the connector generates records rather than reading from a real system.

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: The E2E Testing source requires no authentication. It is a mock source that generates synthetic data for testing the Airbyte pipeline, so it has no secret fields and needs no API key, token, or credentials.

setupSteps.title: Add the E2E Testing source
setupSteps.body: In your Airbyte workspace, create a new source and select E2E Testing. No credentials are requested because the connector produces synthetic data and connects to no external service.

setupSteps.title: Configure the source
setupSteps.body: Set the configuration options for the source as described in the Airbyte E2E Testing documentation for your connector version. Follow the documented field set, since the exact configuration depends on the version of the connector you have installed.

setupSteps.title: Test and save the connection
setupSteps.body: Run the connection test and save the source. Because the source is a self-contained mock generator, the test validates the supplied configuration rather than connectivity to any external endpoint.
